description: §17.1.1 — `else` on a SIBLING element (the legal chain) does NOT fire E-CTRL-005, and `if=` coexisting with `show=` on one element is explicitly legal per §17.1.1's Interaction Notes. Twinned with ctrl-005-else-and-if-same-element-pos: identical structure, the ONLY difference is that `else` sits on a sibling rather than on the `if=` element itself. Doubles as the guard that the E-CTRL-005 check does not over-fire on the legal same-element `if=`+`show=` composition. W-PROGRAM-SPA-INFERRED co-fires incidentally at severity info; not asserted.
rationale: §17.1.1: E-CTRL-005 rejects ONLY the co-occurrence of `else`/`else-if=` with `if=` on a single element. Two distinct rules keep this case silent: (1) `else` on a sibling is the canonical chain form; (2) '`if=`, `else-if=`, and `else` MAY coexist on the same element with `show=`. ... The two attributes compose without conflict.' So a same-element `if=`+`show=` SHALL NOT be rejected.
